_comment: Kitsoki concept pitch — silent ~70s cut. Sells docs/concept.md.

scenes.title: Kitsoki
scenes.subtitle: A conversation engine with deterministic flow

scenes.body: Two extremes dominate today's interactive software.
scenes.lede: Traditional CLIs are unforgiving. Chat agents are unpredictable.

scenes.title: Two extremes

panels.label: Traditional CLI
panels.ascii:
$ ./tool --service=frontend
         --replicas=3
         --region=us-east-1

error: missing required flag --env

# rigid, unforgiving,
# every keystroke matters

panels.label: Chat agent
panels.ascii:
> scale the frontend

  ... thinking ...
  ... tool: read_repo
  ... tool: list_services
  ... tool: ???

# vague, opaque,
# every decision is LLM judgement

scenes.caption: Predictable but rigid — or forgiving but unpredictable.

scenes.lede: The runtime is in charge. The LLM is called when interpretation is needed — and only then.

scenes.title: Control inversion

panels.label: Typical agent
panels.caption: LLM holds the plan; runtime executes.

panels.label: Kitsoki
panels.caption: Runtime holds the plan; LLM is called as a sub-task.

scenes.caption: The arrow that matters is the LLM's return arrow — a typed intent, not a free-form action.

scenes.title: On every turn

turns.user: show me what's open assigned to me

scenes.caption: Deterministic layers first; the LLM is called only when they miss.

scenes.label: of turns route deterministically — without calling the LLM.
scenes.detail: Measured across the cloak, oregon-trail and dev-story stories.

scenes.tagline: Predictable by construction. Interpretive where it has to be.
